POTUS: "The Hormuz Strait is OPEN, and will remain OPEN, with or without Iran. We are reinstating the THE IRANIAN BLOCKADE, so named because it is only stopping Iran’s ships or customers from entering or leaving. All other countries will have fair and open use of the Strait. The U.S.A. will be, from this point forward, known as “THE GUARDIAN OF THE HORMUZ STRAIT,” but as such, and as a matter of FAIRNESS, will be reimbursed, at the rate of 20% on all cargo shipped, for any and all costs necessary to do the job of providing safety and security to this very volatile section of the World. The process and formation will begin immediately. Thank you for your attention to this matter! President DONALD J. TRUMP"

RT by @no_itsmyturn: A strategic shift is underway. Here’s the short version: with the ceasefire gone, Washington can now negotiate from a position of strength. Trump declared on Friday that negotiations will continue despite telling Iran “in no uncertain terms” that the ceasefire is over. The Treasury Department then moved to sanction a key regime financier and several exchange houses that help the regime move billions of dollars, while CENTCOM fired on 100+ Iranian military targets. Whatever remains of the MOU, the two sides are now negotiating amid active hostilities — and that gives Washington greater leverage to raise the military and economic costs of Iranian aggression.

RT by @no_itsmyturn: Strait of Hormuz traffic turns defensive Vessel activity through the Strait of Hormuz declined sharply over 10–12 July, with confirmed crossings down by around 52% week on week, according to #MarineTraffic data. Traffic also shifted back toward more defensive routing patterns. Use of the Iranian and Dark routes increased, while activity on the IMO and Omani corridors fell to minimal levels, indicating that operators continue to prioritise perceived security over more direct transit options. Although no new IMO-confirmed physical attacks have been reported since 27 June, renewed US-Iran tensions and recent IRGC warnings continue to weigh on confidence, reinforcing a cautious approach to commercial shipping through the region.
